name: PT Bank Central Asia Tbk
summary: Gold standard of Indonesian banking. CASA 84.2% moat widening, FCF Bank +Rp46.9T, trading at P/E 12.95x — historically cheap on macro fear.
strengths: CASA 84.2% — highest Indonesia, widening 7 years straight — structural cost-of-fund moat.
FCF Bank +Rp46.9T — cash-compounding franchise; FCF yield on equity 16.7%.
ROE 20.43% — best KBMI-4; consistent earnings growth across all economic cycles.
Fee income 31% of net profit — non-interest diversification via 4.97M credit cards.
risks: Revenue growth +4.34% compressed by weak consumer credit; auto loans -13.3%.
CKPN provision doubled to Rp4.0T — macro precaution; not yet a credit quality alarm.
Macro fear (IDR, BBM) may keep price compressed below fair value longer than expected.

name: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia (Persero) Tbk
summary: Fair price, NPL still rising. Wait for credit cycle to peak before entering.
strengths: Unmatched rural reach — 5,086 BRI Units + mobile/ship terraces, 34.5M ultra-micro borrowers.
NIM 7.8% structurally highest in KBMI-4; micro segment captive pricing.
CASA improving 67.3% → 70.6%, structural funding cost advantage.
risks: NPL 3.10% — joint highest in KBMI-4, peak not yet confirmed.
FCF Bank crushed in 2025 (IDR 17T vs IDR 57T net income) due to corporate push.
LDR 94.2% above OJK ideal range, liquidity buffer thin.
Net income declining 5.8% YoY, CKPN +16.9%.

name: PT Bank Mandiri (Persero) Tbk
summary: KBMI-4 with best-in-class NPL (1.13%) trading at P/BV 1.28x — below historical low. CASA decline + recurring-NP softness offset by Danantara backstop and macro-fear discount. Q1 2026 reported NP includes ~Rp 26T BRIS deconsol paper gain (one-off).
strengths: Best NPL in KBMI-4 (1.13%) — corporate book quality intact through FY 2025.
FCF Bank recovered to Rp 42.7T (yield on equity 13%); RWA growth moderated vs FY 2024.
P/BV 1.28x sits below historical low (1.5x) — macro-fear discount in price.
KBMI-4 + Danantara backstop = lowest deposit/bankruptcy risk in IDX banking.
LDR 85.6% back inside OJK 78–92% ideal range (was 92.6% FY 2024).
risks: CASA structural decline 79.4% → 70.9% — funding mix deteriorating; time deposits +78% YoY.
OpEx +15.3% YoY (promo + employee allowances) — BOPO deteriorating.
Recurring Q1 2026 NP forecast ≈ -7.4% YoY (ex-BRIS-paper-gain); reported +169% misleading.
RPT deposit concentration leapt 14.8% → 22.49% (Himbara government deposits) — rotation risk.
BRIS deconsolidation Q1 2026 = ~Rp 26T paper gain (non-cash); recurring laba drops Rp 2.4T/yr.

name: PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk
summary: Lowest NIM among Big-4 (3.8%) due to SOE-corporate tilt. Profit fell 7.2% YoY despite 16% loan growth. Priced at fair value — zero MOS.
strengths: Credit quality healing: NPL 4.3% (2020) → 1.9% (2025); LAR 28.7% → 8.5%.
KBMI-4 systemic bank — implicit state backstop, stable franchise.
CAR 21.8% well-capitalised; fee income +13.4% YoY.
risks: NIM structural compression: 4.81% (2022) → 3.8% (2025) — SOE corp loans yield only 6.4%.
Net profit -7.2% YoY despite 15.9% loan growth — volume can no longer offset margin.
RPT deposits 27.9% of total — concentrated state-entity funding, rotation risk.
MOS essentially zero at IDR 3,770; fair value IDR 3,200–3,400 range.

name: PT Bank OCBC NISP Tbk
summary: BUY refreshed against FY 2025 + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 db). Commonwealth integration absorbed cleanly; equity grew +19% to Rp 43.86T. CASA recovered to all-time high 58.0% (vs BMRI structural decline). MOS widened +11.8% → +18.7% (above 15% threshold). P/E 6.13× FY 2025 / 5.29× Q1 2026 forward; P/BV 0.71×. NPL ticked 1.6→1.9% — sub-2% acceptable but watch.
strengths: CASA recovered to 58.0% FY 2025 — opposite of BMRI structural decline; demand deposits +59% in 9 months drove the lift.
Commonwealth integration absorbed cleanly: assets +22%, equity +19% over 7 quarters; bargain-purchase noise washed out.
MOS widened +11.8% → +18.7% — crossed canonical 15% threshold for confident BUY.
P/BV 0.71× sits below KBMI-3 corp-comm structural floor — supports MOS.
FCF Bank Rp 3.59T FY 2025 (vs Rp 877B FY 2024) — RWA growth moderated.
NPL still <2% (1.9%); LAR 7.5%; conservative posture intact.
Low RPT (loans 0.04%, deposits 0.59%) — well below 1% flag.
OCBC Bank Singapore institutional anchor; family continuity (Surya Udaya).
risks: NPL ticked up 1.6% → 1.9% FY 2025 — DV Insight flagged asset quality as negative; trajectory matters.
LDR underdeployed ~68% post-deposit-surge — FY 2026 deployment rate watch; either NIM compression (under-deployed) or NPL pressure (over-deployed).
NIM mid-pack — corp-comm focus = structurally lower than retail peers.
KBMI-3 mid-scale = less moat than KBMI-4 (BCA/BMRI/BBRI/BBNI).
Forward forecast assumes CASA tailwind sustains; if demand-deposit lift normalises, NIM expansion thesis weakens.
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 db); Aug 2024 webinar narrative

name: PT Cisarua Mountain Dairy Tbk
summary: Good business at wrong price. FY 2025 NP +33.8% YoY, operating margin EXPANDED 18.5%→21.1%, debt-free, capex stepping up. But mcap Rp 35.7T vs conservative FV Rp 10.9-12.9T = ~+200% premium (was -374% at source). Premium narrowed but still deep. Buy zone Rp 1,140-1,625.
strengths: FY 2025 operating margin EXPANDED 18.5% → 21.1% on G&A discipline + finance income.
NP +33.8% YoY outpaced revenue +18.8% — operating leverage delivered.
Debt-free balance sheet preserved.
Capex stepped up +18.7% YoY — productive deployment of cash.
Finance income +56% YoY — cash + securities deployed at higher yields.
Niche yogurt leadership (Cimory); founder-led + General Atlantic anchor.
DV Insight 5 positive / 1 negative — strongest bullish balance in case-study set.
risks: Premium to conservative FV still ~+200% even after price correction.
USD/IDR exposure on imported milk powder & beef.
ICBP-scale entrant could disrupt yogurt moat.
FY 2025 dividend payout Rp ~2T essentially equal to NP — equity flat YoY.
Inventory +32% YoY — could be FX hedge or expanded production; watch CCC.
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 db); source-recording webinar narrative

name: PT Hartadinata Abadi Tbk
summary: Watch list — not buy. Gross margin collapse + heavy debt-funded inventory. Price drifted Rp 2,780 → 2,930; MOS deepened -265% → -286%. Customer concentration revised top-3 = ~71% (Pegadaian via two entities 57% + BSI 14%); old top-2 read at 57% understated risk.
strengths: LBMA certification potential as catalyst.
2025 OCF turnaround after 6 negative years.
Gold inventory build benefits from gold rally tailwind.
Q1 2026 forecast Moderate +115% YoY NP (gold price tailwind continued).
risks: Gross margin collapse 9.4% → 4.3% with no floor.
D/A balloon 28.9% → 74.4% — leverage on a declining-margin business.
Inventory = 65.6% of total assets, mostly debt-funded.
Cash-buy-slow-sell quirk amplifies WC drag in gold rallies.
Customer concentration top-3 = 71% (Pegadaian government counterparty).
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 db); Mar 2026 webinar narrative

name: PT Bank Danamon Indonesia Tbk
summary: Fair price, FCF Bank negative. Tab 14 retrofitted via canonical bank haircut schedule (precedent: NISP, BMRI, BNGA): NAV Rp 34.8T + 5yr forward Rp 5-8T = FV Rp 39.8-42.8T vs mcap Rp 39.4T → MOS +1% to +8%. P/BV 0.68× looks cheap but FCF Bank negative offsets. Buy zone Rp 3,060-3,500.
strengths: MUFG 92.5% ownership — stable foreign parent, no promoter/governance risk.
ADMF consumer financing moat: ~39% of interest income from only 12% of assets.
NPL 1.7% — best in KBMI-3 class, improving from 1.9% in 2024.
risks: CASA falling structurally (42.3% vs 63.4% peak 2022) → cost of funds rising.
FCF Bank negative 2 years running — all profit absorbed by RWA growth.
LDR 95.5%, above OJK ideal (78–92%) — limited lending room.
ADMF dependency: 32% of loan book, any ADMF deterioration = BDMN deterioration.

name: PT Bank Syariah Indonesia Tbk
summary: Dominant syariah bank (46% market share), FCF Bank positive. Premium valuation at P/BV 1.67x — let it come to you.
strengths: 46.1% Islamic banking market share in financing — structural monopoly position.
FCF Bank positive (+Rp2.65T) — generates real free cash unlike BDMN.
NPF 1.81% improving since 2021 post-merger peak (2.93%).
Gold financing +78% YoY — unique product with Rahn collateral moat.
risks: FaR 9.2% elevated — Rp40.2T Sumatra disaster exposure; watch Q2 2026 data.
Danantara governance: BMRI stake transferred Jan 2026; strategic direction uncertain.
P/BV 1.67x — market already pricing growth; no margin of safety at current price.
G&A high (28.7% of gross revenue) — depreciation from expansion; normalization needed.

name: PP London Sumatera Indonesia Tbk
summary: BUY refreshed against FY 2025 + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 db). CPO upcycle delivered cleanly: NP +148% over 2 years (FY 2023 Rp 761B → FY 2025 Rp 1,886B); cash hoard +60% to Rp 7.6T; debt-free intact. BUT price doubled Rp 825 → Rp 1,670. MOS compressed +45% → +22-27%. No longer the 'cash > mcap, business free' setup; now a 'comfortable discount + cyclical late-stage' read. Buy zone Rp 1,400-1,600.
strengths: CPO upcycle delivered: NP +148% over 2 years; revenue +31.5%; GP margin 27.5% → 41.0%.
Debt-free balance sheet intact — survived El Niño 2023 trough into FY 2025 cycle peak.
Cash hoard +60% to Rp 7.60T — discipline retained through upcycle (dividends maintained, no over-payout).
Cash share of mcap still 67% — investor still gets most of the CPO business at deep discount.
Finance income +27% YoY (Rp 276B FY 2025) — cash deployed productively.
Q1 2026 forecast +27% YoY NP — cycle still positive (late but not yet inflecting).
Tree age 49% in productive 7–20yr band — no replant capex wave imminent.
risks: Price doubled Rp 825 → Rp 1,670; MOS compressed +45% → +22-27%. Deep-discount setup gone.
GP margin compressed 43.7% → 41.0% on rising harvest costs + external TBS purchases (xlsx-flagged late-cycle signals).
CPO ASP late-cycle — MYR/ton trend reversal would mark cycle peak.
RPT to SIMP — fairness must continue to be checked; FY 2025 RPT % not surfaced in xlsx snapshot.
Investment in associates Rp 1,263B → Rp 341M reclassification — confirm via FY 2025 audited notes (likely accounting recategorisation, not disposal).
Salim group concentration risk.
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 db); Mar 2024 MOS webinar narrative

name: PT Adira Dinamika Multi Finance Tbk
summary: Largest multifinance by new bookings (IDR 43.2T), backed by MUFG via BDMN. Profitable but NI declining -26.7% CAGR over 7 years. Market cap above fair value — wait for cheaper entry.
strengths: Largest multifinance by new financing (IDR 43.2T FY2025); 877-office network, 66% outside Java.
BDMN/MUFG parent: unlimited joint financing facility + USD 100M MUFG term loan (Nov 2025).
NPF 2.0% improving; NIM 17.3% typical high-margin for consumer finance.
MFIN merger adds scale without integration friction (affiliated absorb).
risks: Net income CAGR -26.7% (2019–2025) — structural earnings compression over 6 years.
Market share declining: motorcycle 11.8%→8.5%, car 4.8%→4.0%.
Automotive market weak: motorcycle +1% YoY, car sales subdued.
MPL mix shift (IDR 13.3T) — highest-yield but highest-risk; NPF risk rising.

name: PT Bank Raya Indonesia Tbk
summary: Digital transformation failed on credit quality. NPL 14.02% (worst among all IDX banking peers), net loss FY2025, negative retained earnings -IDR 3.2T. Revenue -56.6% CAGR over 7 years. Business gate FAIL.
strengths: BRI parent backing (86.85%) — solvency not at risk, CAR 40.25%.
Raya App 977K users — digital acquisition infrastructure exists.
NIM ~10.1% — digital lending spread high (if credit quality were clean).
risks: NPL 14.02% — worst among all 20 IDX banking peers; digital loan credit quality collapsed.
Net loss FY2025 -IDR 498B; FCF Bank negative; two loss years out of 7.
Retained earnings -IDR 3,223B — all equity from BRI capital injections, not earnings.
Revenue -56.6% CAGR 2019–2025 — structural decline, not cyclical.
Value trap: P/BV 1.49x premium on a bank with destroyed fundamentals (BRI rescue narrative priced in).

name: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk
sectorLabel: Infrastructure — Tower / Telco infra, Jarum Group-controlled
summary: Slow growing but durable archetype. FY 2025 confirms deleveraging thesis: D/E 2.6× → 1.65×, finance cost -1.8% YoY (first decline), FCF margin 38% → 53%, NP +9.4% YoY. Right-issue Q3 2025 absorbed cleanly into balance sheet. Refreshed FV Rp 22-28T vs mcap Rp 24.9T = near fair value with thin premium. Buy zone refreshed Rp 400-440 (was Rp 380).
strengths: Deleveraging thesis materialised FY 2025: D/E 2.6× → 1.65×, finance cost -1.8% YoY (first decline in series).
FCF margin jumped 38% → 53% on lower capex + working-capital release.
Right-issue Q3 2025 (~Rp 5.4T) deployed to debt paydown — purposeful, not chronic dilution.
Independent ownership (Jarum Group) — best-in-class pricing power; ASP/tower 50% above MTEL.
Largest fiber footprint in Indonesia (258K km) — services diversification + contract-bundling moat.
Long-term operator contracts (10+ years) — locked recurring revenue.
Capex declining trajectory FY 2024 → FY 2025 (-27% YoY) — mature-phase optimization confirmed.
Q1 2026 forecast +14.5% NP YoY — accelerating post-deleveraging.
risks: EPS only +3% YoY despite NP +9.4% — right-issue dilution absorbed half the per-share gain.
Tenancy Ratio declining since 2021 peak (1.88× → 1.61×); margin compression structural.
Operator merger contract-roll risk: XLSmart consolidation will compress TR 2027-2030.
Substitute threat (Starlink + KAI rail-fiber) at the margin in Java; outer islands insulated.
Hartono allegation (Nov 2025) — open reputation issue; monitor resolution.
Goodwill Rp 15.8T = 20% of assets — written off entirely in Tab 14 NAV; no liquidation cushion.
MOS thin to none vs reconciled FV; price drift Rp 476 → 488 absorbed the deleveraging FV uplift.
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 db); Mar 2026 webinar narrative

name: PT Astra Otoparts Tbk
summary: Component Supplier with JV Cash Engine archetype. FY 2025 actuals refuted catalyst-fear — NP +7.6% YoY beat forecast 15–27%, GP margin EXPANDED 16.07→16.91% even as BYD/Vinfast pabrik commissioned Mar 2026. Reconciled MOS now -25 to -29% vs Rp 13.4T mcap. Two new flags: dividend paid collapsed Rp 911B→88B FY 2025 + cash hoard at Rp 4.53T = capital-reallocation signal pending. Buy zone widened to Rp 2,150–2,250.
strengths: Pricing power confirmed under live disruption pressure — GP margin EXPANDED 16.07→16.91% in FY 2025 with BYD/Vinfast catalyst on calendar.
FY 2025 NP beat Q3 2025 forecast band (1.85–2.05T) by 15–27% with actual Rp 2.35T.
JV cash engine durable — book value +7.8% YoY; dividend dip (-8.7%) reads as reinvestment cycle, not weakness.
JV-adjusted FCF FY 2025 = Rp 2.10T (+23% YoY); 5-yr avg ratcheted up Rp 1.21T → Rp 1.45T.
Astra Group 80% / Jardine ultimate — multi-decade governance continuity.
OCF positive every year including Covid 2020; FY 2025 OCF +27% YoY.
Production no.1 in IDX auto-component peer set; sticky OEM (AHM 22%, ADM 6% — RPT business-aligned).
Cash hoard Rp 4.53T + dividend-paid collapse Rp 88B = capital-reallocation signal queued.
risks: Vertical-supply-chain disruption catalyst arrived (BYD/Vinfast pabrik Mar 2026) — FY 2025 absorbed it but FY 2026 H1 is the real stress test (lag effect or muted-effect resolution).
Dividend paid collapsed Rp 911B → Rp 88B (-90%) FY 2025 — timing artefact or policy change unknown; cashflow optic concern.
JV dividend received first dip in series (-8.7%) — if persists below Rp 500B/yr signals structural shift.
CBU-import trend elevated (~135K units/yr 2025); imported volume bypasses local sourcing.
Manufacturing-segment margin tipis (~5–6%) — vulnerable to volume drop.
Counterfeit (kawai) goods — tempo-headwind on aftermarket via online channels.
Customer concentration RPT 34.8% revenue — not extractive but exposed to Astra capital allocation.
Cash hoard ambiguity at Rp 4.53T — war chest or opportunity-cost drag depends on deployment direction.
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 db); Nov 2025 webinar narrative

name: PT Indofood Sukses Makmur Tbk
summary: Salim conglomerate (4 segments: Consumer Branded via 80.5% ICBP 61% / Bogasari terigu 20% / Agribusiness IndoAgri-SIMP-LSIP 13% / Distribution 6%). FY 2025: revenue Rp 123.5T (+6.66% YoY), consol NI Rp 15.56T (+19% YoY), parent NI Rp 10.68T after NCI 31.3%, EPS Rp 1,217. P/E TTM 5.67x, P/BV (gross) 0.50x. Crisis-proven controller — Salim's 1997/98 BBCA-vs-INDF triage (sold BBCA, kept INDF) compounded into Pinehill global noodle expansion. Net debt manageable Rp 27.1T (= 22.5% of equity); USD bond stack Rp 45.94T no near-term wall (earliest 2031). Goodwill Rp 56.35T = 25.9% of total assets (Pinehill acquisition premium, zero liquidation value). NCI 31% structural pass-through to ICBP/SIMP public floats — investigated, not extraction. Agent-derived FV Rp 6,600-7,800 via NAV+5yr-FCF haircut (NAV Rp 27.7T + 5yr FCF Rp 55-70T, less 30% NCI claim); cross-checked P/E (consumer 10x × 25% conglomerate discount = 7.5x × parent EPS Rp 900 = Rp 6,750) and P/BV (parent BV Rp 9,585 × 0.7-1.0x = Rp 6,710-9,585). Current Rp 6,900 sits at FV centre — fair, not bargain. Buy trigger Rp 5,850 (need ~15% pullback). NOT a value trap — fundamentals intact; lower headline multiple is deserved (NCI dilution + goodwill drag + FX tail). WAIT for macro dislocation (FX/BBM stress) to deliver Fat Pitch entry.
metrics: Sold BBCA, kept INDF — correct 30 yr later
strengths: Crisis-proven controller (Salim 1997/98 triage); 30 years compounding into Pinehill global mie instan reach.
Multi-segment integrated moat — Bogasari terigu + ICBP brands + IndoAgri upstream naturally hedged across commodity cycle.
Pricing power present at ~60% of revenue (CBP/Indomie brand layer); commodity hedge from IndoAgri offsets ICBP input-cost spikes.
ROE 12.9% consol / 13–15% historical band consistent; FCF Rp 14.3T (FCF/NI 92% — high cash conversion).
Net debt manageable (22.5% of equity); USD bond servicing has 6+ years of runway to first maturity 2031.
risks: Goodwill 25.9% of assets (Pinehill acquisition premium) — zero liquidation value; if Pinehill underperforms, goodwill impairment hits parent equity.
USD bond stack Rp 45.94T — partial natural hedge via Pinehill USD revenue but full hedge ratio unverified; FX tail risk asymmetric.
NCI 31% structural — public floats in ICBP/SIMP dilute parent-attributable economics by ~30%.
Successor (post-Anthoni Salim) crisis-handling untested; first major macro shock under new gen is open watch-item.
40% of revenue (Bogasari + Agri) sits in commodity-price-taker territory; Indonesia manufacturing GDP share weakening (consumer macro headwind).
Current Rp 6,900 sits at centre of FV band — wait for ~15% pullback to Rp 5,850 buy trigger.
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 db); Letter #014 macro frame

name: PT Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur Tbk
sectorLabel: Consumer / FMCG — Branded Foods (Salim Group, INDF subsidiary)
summary: Pure-branded consumer layer of Salim's food empire. Indomie #2 globally (~11.6% share). FY 2025: revenue Rp 74.85T (+3.1% YoY — slowdown vs 7yr CAGR 10%), NI Rp 10.76T (+22% YoY), EPS Rp 791. 7yr Net Profit CAGR 12.7%. Margin discipline holding: op margin 16.8% (2018) → 22.2% (2025). 2022 wheat/CPO cost shock compressed GP% to 33.6% then recovered to 37% (2023-2024) — Letter #014 narrative confirmed. CRITICAL CORRECTION to prior letter-anchored thesis: Pinehill USD bonds are at ICBP level (Rp 45.94T), NOT INDF parent. Finance cost Rp 3-4T/yr drag. Goodwill Rp 54T = 73% of equity (Pinehill acquisition burden). 50.67% of revenue via Indomarco Adi Prima (Salim-related distributor) — structural channel concentration. Agent-derived FV Rp 7,500-9,500 via consumer-multiple lens (P/E 10-13x EPS 791-836; binding per ULTJ doctrine) discounted 8% for Pinehill FX + goodwill risk. Current Rp 6,950 below buy trigger Rp 7,600 → BUY zone. P/E forward 8.3x and P/BV 1.10x both compressed below consumer normal (10-13x, 1.5-2.0x).
metrics: 54.7% — strong underlying return on tangible capital
USD bond exposure (Pinehill at ICBP level)
PT Indomarco Adi Prima (Salim) 50.67% of revenue
strengths: Indomie globally #2 instant noodle franchise (~11.6% share); generational brand (est. 1972), >60% Indonesia share. Strongest mass-market consumer moat on IDX.
Down-trading defensive — mie instan is the canonical destination of crisis consumption reallocation (Letter #014, macro_context §5).
Margin discipline through scale: op margin 16.8% (2018) → 22.2% (2025) = +5 ppt expansion; A&P only 3.4% of revenue (vs MYOR 7.1%, peers 3.5%) reflects established brand pull.
FCF Rp 8-10T/yr stable; OCF Rp 12T/yr; dividend payout 27% conservative — internal funding capacity intact.
Salim Group governance proven through 1997/98 crisis (parent-level BBCA divestment to recap INDF debt).
Pinehill MENA/Africa distribution provides international diversification + export FX revenue (30% of total).
Statistically cheap by consumer norms: P/E 8.3x forward (vs 10-13x normal envelope); P/BV 1.10x (vs 1.5-2x normal); MOS +27% vs FV high.
risks: Pinehill USD bonds (Rp 45.94T) at ICBP level — direct FX P&L exposure; finance cost Rp 3-4T/yr drag (2022 spiked to Rp 6.18T on IDR depreciation).
Goodwill Rp 54T = 73% of equity, tied to Pinehill operations; impairment risk if MENA/Africa operations deteriorate. 25% impairment = Rp 13.5T equity write-down.
Channel concentration: PT Indomarco Adi Prima (Salim-related) accounts for 50.67% of revenue. Structural distribution intermediation, not extraction, but arm's-length RPT pricing must be verified.
Revenue growth slowing — FY 2025 +3.1% YoY is slowest since 2018; Letter #014 macro frame (middle-class fiscal pressure → mass-discretionary slowdown) visible.
Pricing power moderate — mass-market mie instan can pass through ~70% of commodity cost-push; 30% absorbed in margin (2022 wheat/CPO spike compressed GP% to 33.6%).
Salim succession unproven for next generation (Anthoni Salim ~76; Axton/Welirang generation not yet stress-tested through a crisis).
NCI elevated at 12.5% of pre-NCI profit — just below 15% red-flag threshold per universal SKILL; watch sustained level.

name: PT Ultrajaya Milk Industry & Trading Company Tbk
summary: WAIT against FY 2025 + Q1 2026 forward cite (Mar 2026 db, first full xlsx ingest). UHT category leader at 34% share (vs ICBP 12%, CMRY 7%); net-cash (Rp 3.16T cash, debt Rp 1.6B); highest dairy net margin 13.0%. FY 2025 revenue -1.2% YoY (first contraction since COVID) flagged by DV Insight as Negative; margin expansion +290bps driven by -25% selling-expense cut, not gross margin. P/BV 2.21x above the 2x normal consumer ceiling; FV Rp 1,300-1,600/share (P/E 10-13x). Buy zone Rp 1,200 (1.5x BV crisis floor). No MOS at current price.
metrics: 16-17 kg/yr vs Malaysia/India >50 kg (3x structural upside)
strengths: UHT category leadership 34% share — almost 3x ICBP, 5x CMRY. Vertical integration (own dairy herd 6,225 head FY25).
Highest net margin among dairy peers (13.0% vs ICBP 12.1%, DMND 3.7%).
Net-cash BS intact through FY 2022 commodity-cost cycle. Bank loan Rp 1.6B vs cash Rp 3.16T.
Survived 97/98 Krismon as smaller player + 2022 commodity shock — operational durability confirmed.
Structural runway: Indonesia 16-17 kg/capita dairy vs Malaysia/India >50 kg = 3x economic-driven upside as middle class expands.
New factory under construction (DV Insight #4 Mar 2026) — articulated runway for retained earnings.
DV Insight 3 positive / 1 negative / 1 neutral — bullish lean (vertical integration, A&P discipline, MBG program support).
risks: FY 2025 revenue -1.2% YoY = first contraction since COVID; need 1-2 quarters to confirm cycle-low vs cycle-turn.
Margin uplift FY 2025 driven by selling-expense -25%, not gross margin — A&P reversion risk as new factory needs demand creation.
P/BV 2.21x sits above the Letter #014 normal 2x ceiling for ULTJ/ICBP. No MOS at current price.
Imported milk powder + Tetra Pak (USD-linked) = FX exposure; hedging program disclosure absent in xlsx.
Family control 81.7% (Prawiradjaja); governance counterweight thin; dividend policy serves family cash needs.
FY 2022 negative FCF (-Rp 115B) proves cyclical FCF sensitivity to commodity input shocks.
Modern-trade channel pricing power (Indomaret, Alfamart) compresses FMCG supplier margins (sector-wide constraint).

name: PT Gudang Garam Tbk
summary: Indonesia's #2 legal cigarette manufacturer; Wonowidjojo family 76% control (via Suryaduta + Suryamitra + direct stakes). Severe cycle: revenue Rp 124.7T peak (2022) → Rp 89.4T (2025) -28%; NP Rp 10.88T peak (2019) → Rp 0.98T trough (2024) -91%. FY 2025: revenue -9.4% YoY, NP +59% YoY to Rp 1.56T, EPS Rp 809. Q3 2025 quarterly NP Rp 0.99T = 64% of full year — possible Menkeu Purbaya enforcement effect inflection but Value team Q1 2026 forecast (Rp 380-420B NP) does NOT extrapolate. SKM-heavy 87.8% mix (vs HMSP 56.2%) concentrates Gol I cukai burden. FX-insulated (raw materials domestic — Letter #014). Bad cash-mgmt archetype: Rp 15.6T in 'Other non-current assets' (bandara/tol non-core capital trap); 2024-2025 partial correction (capex stepped down Rp 5T → 2.48T; bank loans Rp 9T → 0.76T heavy deleveraging) but no formal divestment. BVPS Rp 32,520 → P/BV 0.51x deeply below book. Letter #014 cited price Rp 8,500 → market already +96% in <10 days. Agent-derived FV Rp 16,000-22,000 (synthesis: Net Asset lens supportive Rp 22-27K; going-concern earnings power lens stretched Rp 8-15K — bifurcation = value-trap-or-bargain dilemma). Verdict WAIT — price Rp 16,625 between buy trigger Rp 15,400 and FV high Rp 22,000.
metrics: SKM 87.8% + SKT 9.5% (vs HMSP SKM 56.2% / SKT 34.1% — GGRM Gol I cukai concentrated)
NP Rp 0.99T alone (64% of full year) — Purbaya enforcement signal?
Bad — Non-Core Capital Trap (Rp 15.6T bandara/tol; partially correcting 2024-2025)
Structurally insulated (raw materials domestic — unique among consumer)
Bank loans Rp 9T+ (2023) → Rp 0.76T (2025); capex stepped down Rp 5T → Rp 2.48T
~Rp 2-3T/yr (modest); FY 2025 OCF Rp 11.6T driven by inventory unwind (not sustainable)
strengths: FX-insulated — raw materials (tembakau, cengkeh) domestic; no IDR-collapse exposure. Unique structural advantage among consumer emiten.
Demand floor structural — addictive product, persistent consumption pattern; survives within retained loyalist segment.
Brand premium retains price-insensitive customer base — top 20-30% of revenue is defensible.
2024-2025 capital discipline returning: heavy deleveraging (bank loans -92%); capex stepped down (non-core deployment tapering); FY 2025 dividend resumed (Rp 0.96T vs zero in FY 2024).
P/BV 0.51x deeply below book — statistically cheap; downside cushioned by net-asset value floor.
Q3 2025 quarterly NP spike (Rp 0.99T alone) — possible Menkeu Purbaya enforcement inflection signal (TBD whether sustainable).
Inventory unwind (Rp 7T drawdown FY 2025) drove OCF Rp 11.61T — real cash generation despite weak NP.
risks: Bad-archetype Non-Core Capital Trap: Rp 15.6T in 'Other non-current assets' = bandara + jalan tol concessions. Fails 2/3 CEO duty tests (Letter #014 verbatim). 2024-2025 partial correction (no fresh non-core deployment) but EXISTING balance unaddressed.
Cukai treadmill structural: GP% 20.6% (2019) → 10.1% (2025); SKM 87.8% mix concentrates Gol I tariff burden — vs HMSP's SKT-heavier shelter and WIIM's Gol II cost advantage.
Volume migration to illegal cigarettes (rokok ilegal merajalela) — addictive demand persists but volume + margin both compressed. Down-trade inside tobacco.
Value team Q1 2026 forecast (Rp 380-420B NP, annualized Rp 1.5-1.7T) does NOT extrapolate Q3 2025 spike forward → conservative view from the source — implies Q3 was partly one-off.

Value-trap risk per Letter #013: cheap P/BV reflects structural cukai pressure + capital allocation problem; resolution requires catalyst (illegal-cig enforcement effectiveness + formal non-core divestment), neither yet confirmed.
Wonowidjojo family decisions unilateral; no foreign-parent governance discipline (unlike HMSP-Philip Morris); no institutional block at 23.8% public float to push capital reform.
ESG-rated lowest; mainstream institutional foreign investors largely excluded — structural valuation discount may persist regardless of fundamentals.
Q4 2025 implied NP Rp 0.45T (vs Q3 Rp 0.99T) suggests Q3 had one-off component; sustainable run-rate likely Rp 1.5-2.5T/yr — well below 2019 peak Rp 10.88T.
Price already rallied +96% from Letter #014 cite Rp 8,500 to current Rp 16,625 in <10 days — much of catalyst hope may be priced in.

name: PT Bank CIMB Niaga Tbk
summary: P/E 6.82x, P/BV 0.81x, FCF yield 12.3% — agent-derived FV Rp 2,000–2,500 vs market Rp 1,865. CASA 70% best-in-KBMI-3, NPL 1.81%, CoC 0.74%. Foreign-parent strategic-optionality risk + NIM compression are watch-items.
strengths: CASA 70% — best-in-KBMI-3, +14.7pp over 5 years, narrowing gap to KBMI-4 averages.
Asset quality top quartile: NPL 1.81%, LAR 6.9% (improved from 8.6%), CoC 0.74%.
Positive FCF Bank IDR 5.77T (FCF yield 12.3%, well above 7% cheap threshold).
CIMB Group regional franchise — ASEAN cross-border trade-finance flow.
Sharia financing growth engine: 11.6% (2018) → 19.9% (2024) loan share.
risks: NIM structural compression: 5.31% (2019) → 3.97% (2025), -134bps over 6 years.
Foreign-parent (CIMB Group Malaysia 91.5%) strategic-optionality risk vs family-controlled peers.
Revenue growth muted: +21.6% over 7 years (~2.8%/yr CAGR).
CoC 0.74% near floor — limited room for further provisioning compression; future NI growth needs volume.
Treasury earnings dependency: FY2025 NI included IDR 2.17T from securities sales (~31% of NI).

name: PT Bank Tabungan Negara (Persero) Tbk
sectorLabel: Banking — KBMI-3 / KPR specialist (state-owned via Danantara)
summary: KBMI-3 KPR (mortgage) monoline, state-owned via Danantara (60%). FY 2025: NII +58% YoY (BI rate cut benefit, NIM recovery from 38.9% spread → 50.1%) but CKPN +212% YoY (normalization after artificially low FY 2024) → reported NI flat Rp 3.5T. Asset quality: NPL 3.1% improving, LAR 19.4% worst in peer set (structural KPR long-duration). Funding mix deteriorating: CASA 56.5% → 50.4%. Pricing power WEAK (KPR rate cap'd by FLPP subsidi + competitive 8.0% SBDK floor). P/BV 0.54x statistically cheap but reflects mediocre 9.7% ROE. Agent-derived FV Rp 800-1,400 (Net Assets tab BLANK in xlsx; canonical bank haircut schedule applied, Component 1 NAV ~Rp 413/sh + Component 2 5yr FCF Bank avg ~Rp 428/sh). Verdict WAIT — price Rp 1,380 in dead zone between buy trigger Rp 980 and sell trigger Rp 1,540.
metrics: 48.5% of book → growing dependence on FLPP
strengths: Durable state-backed franchise — survived Krismon, GFC, COVID; Danantara 60% controlling stake = policy alignment with national housing program.
Dominant subsidi-KPR specialist (~40%+ FLPP market share); niche moat in government-backed mortgage channel.
NIM recovery FY 2025 (+58% NII YoY) confirms BI rate cycle benefit — structural rate-sensitivity asset.
P/BV 0.54x = statistically cheap; downside cushioned by tangible asset backing (Rp 2,580 BVPS).
State capital injection 2025 deployed to loan growth (loans +Rp 40T) not SBN parking — policy intent intact.
risks: Pricing power WEAK — KPR rate (SBDK Housing 8.0%) cap'd by FLPP subsidi program + competitive floor; cannot pass cost-push through to borrowers (Letter #013 doctrine).
LAR 19.4% worst in peer set — structural KPR long-duration cumulative stress + ~16 ppt restructured/special-mention exposure; tightening loan haircut to 90% makes NAV negative.
CASA declining trajectory (56.5% → 50.4%) = funding cost structurally elevated; further decline pressures NIM.
ROE 9.67% mediocre; EPS effectively flat over 7 years (Rp 218 → Rp 249) → growth thesis tired.
Borrower segment macro risk: subsidi KPR (48% of book) is low-income; non-subsidi exposed to middle-class fiscal pressure (Letter #014 frame) — DSR deterioration possible in macro stress.
CKPN volatility (FY 2024 Rp 1.98T → FY 2025 Rp 6.18T) makes reported NI an unreliable steady-state proxy.
CAR 19.02% just below ideal 20%; future CKPN spike could pressure capital buffer.

name: PT Semen Indonesia (Persero) Tbk
summary: Indonesia cement market leader (50.5% share, 47.4% capacity) but earnings collapsed -93% over 5 years (NI Rp 2.7T 2020 → Rp 0.18T FY 2025). FY 2025 revenue Rp 35.24T (-2.6% YoY); GP margin compressed 22.0% → 20.1%; net margin 0.51%. EPS Rp 28 (vs Rp 107 FY 2024 / Rp 380-451 2019-2020 peak). Q1 2026 xlsx forecast: optimistic NI Rp 71B / moderate Rp 33B / **conservative -Rp 5B (LOSS)** — first quarterly-loss forecast in modern history. P/BV 0.30x deeply below book (BV/share Rp 7,068); but P/E 76x trailing / 47-105x forward is unsustainable. **All 5 Letter #013 value-trap signals fire**: statistically cheap + persistent discount + recovery promised never delivered + fundamentals deteriorating + industry overcapacity ~45% structural. ASP Rp 695K/ton is LOWEST among 5 cement peers (INTP Rp 853K = +23%); pricing power weak/absent. State-controlled (Danantara 51.2%) — DMO Coal allocation + IKN preferred-supplier are real edges, but BUMN governance ≠ private capital discipline. Receivables aging stressed (17.8% over 365 days). NOT yet broken-broken: OCF Rp 3.72T positive even in trough; balance sheet healthy (net debt Rp 3.23T = 0.07x assets, deleveraging from Rp 12.7T 2023); CCC compressed 39.7 → 20.5 days. Agent-derived FV Rp 2,400-4,700 (NAV Rp 1,242/share at 45% Fixed Asset haircut + 5yr FCF Rp 1,481-2,963/share cycle-dependent). Current Rp 2,130 below FV floor mathematically, but value-trap doctrine SKIPs. WAIT for confirmed cycle inflection — do NOT buy on book-value cheapness alone.
metrics: Rp 3.72T / Rp 2.62T (positive even in earnings trough)
strengths: Indonesia cement market leader — 50.5% share, 47.4% capacity; high utilisation 71.3% vs industry avg 55%.
DMO Coal allocation = state-protected coal supply at sub-market pricing (cost edge vs INTP/CMNT).
IKN preferred-supplier status provides demand floor for state infrastructure projects.
Healthy balance sheet — net debt 0.07x assets, deleveraging Rp 12.7T 2023 → Rp 7.87T 2025.
Working-capital discipline — CCC compressed 39.7 → 20.5 days over 2 years (DPO 101 days lever).
Positive OCF Rp 3.72T even in earnings trough — D&A Rp 3.24T hides true cash power vs reported NI.
risks: Letter #013 value-trap pattern: 5/5 signals fire (statistically cheap + persistent discount + recovery promised never arrived + fundamentals deteriorating 5 yrs + structural industry overcapacity ~45%).
Earnings collapsed -93% over 5 years (NI Rp 2.7T → Rp 0.18T); revenue down 12% from 2019 peak — structural, not cyclical noise.
Lowest ASP among 5 cement peers (Rp 695K/ton; INTP +23%; SMBR +34%) — pricing power weak/absent.
Q1 2026 xlsx conservative scenario shows Rp -5B LOSS — first quarterly-loss forecast in modern history.
Industry overcapacity ~45% structural; no consolidation runway visible (SMGR has most plants but as state entity is least likely to shut).
BUMN governance overlay — strategic alignment with infrastructure agenda but capital discipline ≠ private-sector; dividend politically constrained.
Receivables 17.8% over 365 days = collection stress (likely state-contractor + property-developer payment delays).
Coal cost dependency 53% intensity — H2 2024 coal spike drove FY 2025 GP-margin compression.
Buy trigger Rp 3,055 mathematically met (current Rp 2,130 < trigger), but value-trap doctrine says SKIP — do NOT buy on book-value cheapness alone.
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 ValueDB xlsx)

name: PT Bukit Asam Tbk
summary: Indonesia's #1 coal-reserves owner (2,934 Mt, 67.8-year mine life — by far longest in peer set; ITMG 17.5, ADRO 26.8, BUMI 30.8). State-controlled via MIND ID/Danantara (65.93%). FY 2025 revenue Rp 42.65T (-0.3% YoY, flat: volume +5.9% offset by ASP -6.0%); GP margin compressed 19.2% → 14.7%; NI Rp 2.96T (-42% YoY, -77% from Rp 12.78T 2022 cycle peak). EPS Rp 254 (vs Rp 1,094 2022 peak). Cash margin per ton Rp 82K vs Rp 477K 2022 peak (-83%) — 5-year low. P/E TTM 11.4x (cycle-trough multiple); P/BV 1.48x mid-cycle (NOT cheap-on-book like SMGR); forward annualised P/E 19-22x (forecast already discounts further decline). Q1 2026 xlsx scenarios all show flat-to-down NI vs Q1 2025. Capex stepped up Rp 1.23T → Rp 3.51T (+185%) funding railway expansion + production capacity; 2026 capex budget Rp 3.64T = elevated regime. Healthy balance sheet: net cash Rp 1.29T; equity Rp 22.6T flat; OCF positive every year in 8-yr window (Rp 3.1T-12.5T range). Dividend yield 11.55% (Rp 335/share). DMO Coal mechanism = PLN captive 33.83% of revenue at regulated price. Successful diversification: China share 31.6% (2021) → 2.2% (2025) replaced by Bangladesh 13.6% + India 7.9% + Vietnam 5.7%. Letter #013 value-trap test: 2/5 weak — PTBA is NOT a value-trap, it is a cyclical commodity with structural reserves moat. Receivables aging HEALTHY (95% within 90 days, vs SMGR 17.8% >365 days). Agent-derived FV Rp 2,200-4,200 (NAV base Rp 711/share + 5yr FCF Rp 868-2,387/share cycle-dependent; Mining Properties sensitivity 40-500% reflects book-vs-economic-reserves asymmetry). Current Rp 2,900 at lower-mid of FV range — fair, not bargain. mosBuy 0.30 trigger Rp 2,940 barely met. WAIT for cycle inflection (cash margin recovery, ASP stability, capex ROI validation).
metrics: 2,934 Mt (#1 in peer set; BYAN 1,692 #2; AADI 1,121 #3)
Rp 6.26T / Rp 2.76T (positive every year in 8-yr window)
NET CASH Rp 1.29T (Cash 4.52T − Bank loan 3.23T; loan +131% YoY)
33.83% of revenue (DMO regulated price; not bankruptcy risk)
strengths: Indonesia's #1 coal-reserves owner — 2,934 Mt reserves, 67.8-year mine life (vs ITMG 17.5, ADRO 26.8, BUMI 30.8). Structural moat that competitors cannot replicate.
Net cash balance sheet Rp 1.29T; equity Rp 22.6T flat over 3 years; OCF positive every year in 8-yr window (Rp 3.1T-12.5T) — never a loss year.
High dividend yield 11.55% (Rp 335/share) with state-mandated payout policy; cushion vs cycle volatility even if cut to Rp 235/share in FY 2026 = still 8.1%.
DMO Coal captive volume floor (~34% revenue via PLN at regulated price); first cross-skill DMO feedback loop in framework (PTBA supplier side ↔ SMGR buyer side).
Dedicated rail logistics moat (Tanjung Enim → Tarahan port, 25 Mt/yr capacity); +20 Mt/yr railway extension 80% complete Dec 2025, COD 2H 2026 — production unlock.
Diversified export book — successfully shifted China 31.6% (2021) → 2.2% (2025) to Bangladesh 13.6% + India 7.9% + Vietnam 5.7% + Korea 4.7% + Philippines 4.2%.
Healthy receivables aging (95% within 90 days; only 5% >90 days) — inverse of SMGR's stressed aging. PLN counterparty pays regularly.
BUMN state guarantee (MIND ID/Danantara 65.93%) — no insolvency risk; energy security mandate keeps state political support during transition.
risks: Cycle vulnerability — NI cycles 5.3× (Rp 2.4T 2020 trough to Rp 12.78T 2022 peak); current cash margin per ton at 5-yr low Rp 82K.
Cash margin per ton compressed -83% from 2022 peak — ASP falling faster than cash cost can adjust (cash cost flat Rp 840K 2024+2025).
Capex stepped up Rp 1.23T → Rp 3.51T (+185%) funded partially with new debt (bank loan +131% YoY); capital-discipline question for BUMN paying 130%-of-NI dividend.
PLN single-customer concentration 33.83% — state-related so not bankruptcy risk but regulatory-pricing risk (DMO price ceiling caps cycle-peak upside).
ESG / energy-transition headwind — Indonesian state coal politically protected but international investor flows likely capped; multiple compression risk on global ESG screens.
Strip ratio rising 3.6x (2017) → 6.07x (2025) — Tanjung Enim mine maturing; future strip will trend higher unless new pits open at scale.
Coal-quality discount — sub-bituminous (3,700-5,300 kcal) vs high-CV thermal peers (ITMG 6,100-6,500, HRUM 5,400-6,400) means structural ASP discount in any cycle phase.
Q1 2026 forecast scenarios all show flat-to-down NI vs Q1 2025; forward annualised P/E 19-22x is NOT cheap if 2026 prints at forecast levels.
BUMN governance — recent capex step-up + dividend 130%-of-NI raises payout-discipline question; political capital allocation may not optimise for shareholder return.
MIND ID Trading Pte Ltd revenue line disappeared in FY 2025 (was 8.5% FY 2024) — RPT re-routing nature unclear, monitor at next disclosure.
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + Q1 2026 forecast (Mar 2026 ValueDB xlsx)

name: PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk
summary: World-class copper-gold porphyry miner — Batu Hijau (active) + Elang (undeveloped). 23.4 Blbs copper reserves = #5 globally. FY2025 transition year: export ban + smelter at 43% utilization compressed revenue -31% YoY. Smelter + concentrator expansion targeting full ramp 2026–2027. Net debt USD 5.76B. MSCI exit (May 2026) dislocation to Rp 3,700 approaches MOS buy trigger Rp 3,750 — verify smelter trajectory before entry.
strengths: World-class copper-gold reserves: 23.4 Blbs copper + 32.8 Moz gold (Batu Hijau + Elang combined) — #5 globally for copper; only PT Freeport Indonesia larger on IDX.

Integrated smelter commissioned 2024 — first in Indonesia for copper cathode + refined gold; eliminates concentrate export dependency long-term; GP margin 45–58% best in class vs ANTM (9.4%), MDKA (7.9%).
100% USD revenue — IDR depreciation is a structural tailwind; BI-Rate macro less relevant than LME copper + LBMA gold.
Elang generational optionality: 17.78 Blbs copper + 26.44 Moz gold undeveloped; any development announcement is a re-rating catalyst.
risks: Heavy leverage: net debt USD 5.76B, debt/asset 60.84% and rising; finance cost USD 386M = 20.9% of FY2025 revenue — dominant earnings drag.
Smelter execution risk: 43–44% utilization FY2025; ramp to 100% contingent on concentrator expansion COD Q3 2026 and no further technical shutdowns.
Regulatory royalty hike: copper 4% → 7–10%, gold 3.75% → 7–16% from April 2025 — USD 219M absorbed FY2025 (11.9% of revenue); further hike risk.
Export quota: ESDM 480,000 dmt permit valid only Oct 2025–Apr 2026; renewal not guaranteed — concentrate revenue at risk without timely extension.
asOf: FY 2025 actuals + FY 2026 forecast scenarios (Mar 2026 ValueDB xlsx)
